rulings.Case Title: RAUL VASQUEZ VS. NATIONAL DEFAULT SERVICING CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday January 17, 2019 line 1. DEFENDANT NATIONAL DEFAULT SERVICING CORPORATION, SELECT PORTFOLIO SERVICING, INC., CITIGROUP MORTGAGE LOAN TRUST INC., U.S. BANK, N.A., DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT continued to February 7, 2019. Moving party is ordered to provide a copy of the operative complaint in compliance with SFLR 2.7B no later than January 25, 2018 with a cover letter reflecting new hearing date. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: BORREGAS COURT ASSOCIATES, A CALIFORNIA GENERAL VS. BOSTON FINANCIAL INVESTMENT MANAGEMENT A ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday January 17, 2019 line 2. DEFENDANT BFIM-CALIFORNIA ACQUISITION CORP., ERRONEOUSLY, EDISON CAPITAL HOUSING INVESTMENTS, EDISON CAPITAL HOUSING PARTNERS, XI, LLC DEMURRER TO 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT OVERRULED. All causes of action are sufficiently alleged. The Court takes the allegations in the operative complaint as true for the purposes of the demurrer. The Court further considers reasonable and feasible interpretations of the contracts as advanced by the Plaintiff for the purposes of this demurrer. Under all of the agreements pertaining to the Projects, Plaintiff has sufficiently alleged that the attempt to exercise the action was timely (See Guaranty, Recital C and Option Agreement, Article II), that Plaintiff could not properly exercise the Option in light of Limited Partner's actions/inactions regarding information requested and appraisal (See Option, Article X), and that Limited Partner is a necessary party to the action in light of interrelated agreements and Option, Article X. The causes of action pertaining to breach of the limited partnership agreement contain sufficient facts to state those causes of action. The determination regarding the transfer cannot be made as a matter of law (without inquiry into the surrounding facts spanning almost 30 years) and therefore is premature for the determination on the demurrer. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: CAROL ANN TRAVLOS VS. JACKIELYN HARRIS ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday January 17, 2019 line 3. DEFENDANT JACKIELYN HARRIS, AMBER MCGEARY DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT OVERRULED. Defendant has 5 days to answer. Complaint alleges a cause of action for unlawful detainer and Defendants have not demonstrated that the Notice of Termination of Tenancy is deficient. = (501/CFH)
